title: (No AI) Claude can now use your 1Password credentials for you
author: Jess Weatherbed
content:
![A robot with the claude logo on its face.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/05/STKB364_CLAUDE_2_C_96d15c.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

1Password has launched a new browser integration for Claude that allows the Anthropic chatbot to access stored security credentials like usernames and passwords. The 1Password for Claude feature means that users can authorize Claude to complete multi-step tasks like booking travel and managing online accounts on their behalf without having to manually input their login credentials, but without actually exposing your security information to Anthropic's AI models, according to 1Password.

That's made possible by a new "zero-exposure security framework" developed by 1Password, which works by injecting the required credentials for each task thro …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/966442/1password-anthropic-claude-browser-integration)

That's made possible by a new "zero-exposure security framework" developed by 1Password, which works by injecting the required credentials for each task thro …

title: (No AI) Google ordered to open Android and Search to rivals in Europe
author: Robert Hart
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/STK450_EU_E-2-1.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Google must give rival AI assistants and search engines greater access to key parts of Android and Google Search after the European Union ordered the company to comply with the bloc's digital antitrust rules.

The two [decisions](https://ec.europa.eu/commission/presscorner/detail/en/ip_26_1634), handed down Thursday, could weaken Google's control over two of the tech industry's most important platforms and have far-reaching consequences for the company, shape the future of its AI tool Gemini, and open up new opportunities for rivals to gain ground. Google has until January 2027 to begin sharing search data and July 2027 to implement changes to Android.

The rulings stem from technical regulatory proceedings …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/policy/966438/eu-google-android-ai-interoperability-search-data-dma)

The two decisions, handed down Thursday, could weaken Google's control over two of the tech industry's most important platforms and have far-reaching consequences for the company, shape the future of its AI tool Gemini, and open up new opportunities for rivals to gain ground. Google has until January 2027 to begin sharing search data and July 2027 to implement changes to Android.
The rulings stem from technical regulatory proceedings …
Read the full story at The Verge.

creator: Reece Rogers
title: (No AI) Please Stop Making Me Opt Out of AI
dc:creator: Reece Rogers
content: I’m sick of “opt-out” toggles for automatically enabled generative AI features. It’s past time to make “opt in” the default setting for sensitive features.
contentSnippet: I’m sick of “opt-out” toggles for automatically enabled generative AI features. It’s past time to make “opt in” the default setting for sensitive features.

creator: Will Knight
title: (No AI) AI Isn’t Smarter Than a Baby—Yet
dc:creator: Will Knight
content: Babies are tremendous learning machines, and key advances for AI may soon be found in the architecture of their little brains.
contentSnippet: Babies are tremendous learning machines, and key advances for AI may soon be found in the architecture of their little brains.
